title: Introduce Yourself to Strangers
summary: Introduce yourself to someone new without feeling awkward.
learnerPromise: Introduce yourself to someone new without feeling awkward.
whyItMatters: First impressions happen fast. A good introduction builds trust and starts relationships on the right foot.
realLifeUses: Meet a new classmate on the first day of school.
Introduce yourself at a job interview.
Meet a friend's parents for the first time.
Join a new club or team and meet members.
youWillLearn: Make eye contact and offer a handshake or wave.
Give a little context about yourself.
Ask the other person about themselves.
miniChallenge: Practice a 15-second introduction out loud, including your name and one context.
steps: Make eye contact and smile as you approach.
Offer a handshake or wave if it feels appropriate.
Say your name clearly: 'Hi, I am [Name].'
Add one piece of context: 'I am new here' or 'I am in your class.'
Ask them something: 'What is your name?' or 'How do you know the host?'
completionCriteria: You successfully introduce yourself to someone new.
The conversation lasts at least thirty seconds.
commonProblems: Not knowing what to say after the introduction.
tips: Repeat the person's name right after they say it.
Stand up straight. Good posture shows confidence.
Have a follow-up question ready in advance.
